column3: Use for integral, inseparable physical features of the object that have structural coherence -- paint layers, ground preparation, surface coatings, joints. Connected to parent via P46 or P56 depending on type.

Use when the part is a separable, human-made object in its own right -- frame, lining canvas, applied label or seal. May warrant a full Heritage Object record if studied independently. Connected to parent via P46 or P198 depending on type.

A physically distinguishable component, feature, or zone of a Heritage Object that requires individual identification or connection to other entities in the knowledge graph. The appropriate class (E22 or S20) is determined by part type.
Document Note: Heritage Object Part Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Heritage Object Part Model Overview
column3: This model defines a Heritage Object Part as any physically distinguishable element of a Heritage Object that a researcher needs to individually identify, describe, or connect to other entities. Parts range from structural components (primary support, ground layer) to surface features (tool marks, inscriptions). The connecting property to the parent Heritage Object (P46, P56, or P198) is determined by part type. Where a part warrants a full metadata profile of its own -- such as a sample or sample site -- a dedicated model should be used instead.
The parent Heritage Object of which this is a part.
Any document, report, or publication in which this part is described or documented.
The material composition of this part, where distinctive from the parent object.
The measured dimensions of this part, where recorded separately from the parent object.
The production event by which this part was made, recorded only where the part was made separately from the parent object -- for example a frame or lining canvas.

A curated physical artefact that contains or carries the sampled matter (S13). Serves as the object-management layer: it holds identifiers, condition records, custody, storage, and images. P46 is used as a pragmatic contains/carries proxy for collection management purposes.

The removed matter treated as an amount of matter (CRMsci S13) whose identity is grounded in material continuity and representativity. Serves as the scientific layer: it is the subject of measurement, observation, modification, and splitting events.
